---
title: "Carmen Galán Acedo"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/carmen.png){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:cgalanac@gmail.com)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**Visiting Researcher**  
UNAM-Morelia

*Interests:* [Landscape ecology]{.profile-tag} [Primatology]{.profile-tag} [Spatial patterns]{.profile-tag}

I obtained my PhD in Biological Sciences from UNAM in 2019. My main research experience has focused on tropical landscape ecology, with a strong conservation biology component. My main objective is to contribute to a better understanding of the effects of land-use change on biodiversity, particularly evaluating its effects on primates at regional and global scales.

### Education

- **PhD** — Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, UNAM-Morelia (2019)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
